description_long: This object is a cluster of white calla lily flowers with broad, lush green leaves radiating outward from the base. The flowers are trumpet-shaped, with smooth, slightly curled petals forming a funnel around a central spadix. The leaves are large, ovate, and have a waxy texture, with visible veins and a gentle undulating surface. The stems are thick, green, and upright, supporting the flowers above the foliage. The overall arrangement is dense and natural, resembling a healthy ornamental plant often found in urban parks or landscaped gardens. The coloration is predominantly green with striking white flowers, creating a visually appealing contrast.
description: A cluster of white calla lily flowers with broad green leaves and upright stems, displaying a lush and ornamental appearance.
description_view_0: This view highlights the central cluster of white flowers surrounded by large, overlapping green leaves.
description_view_1: From this angle, the trumpet-shaped flowers are more prominent, and the arrangement of the leaves appears fuller and more layered.
description_view_2: This side view shows the upright stems and the spatial separation between the flowers, with leaves fanning outward.
description_view_3: The rear view emphasizes the dense foliage and the backs of the leaves, with the flowers partially visible above.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a whorled, funnel-shaped configuration, with each flower displaying a single, large, spirally wrapped petal (spathe) encircling a central spadix. The arrangement is radial and symmetrical, with some overlapping at the edges.
